titleStmt.title: A Transcription of John in Codex Sarzanensis (pars prior)
VL22
The New Testament
The Gospel according to John
john
titleStmt.funder: The Arts and Humanities Research Council

sponsor.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing

publisher.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing
University of Birmingham

availability.p: Licensed under a Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International License (CC BY 4.0): available for re-use provided attribution is made to the original creators (the IGNTP and any named participants) and an indication is given of any changes made. See further .

msItem.title: Euangelia secundum Lucam et Iohannem
Gospels according to Luke and John

listBibl.bibl: G. Godu, Codex Sarzanensis. Fragments d'ancienne version latine du quatrième Evangile (Spicilegium Casinense 2), Monte Cassino, 1936
N. Ghiglione, L'evangeliario purpureo di Sarezzano (sec. V/VI) (Fontes Ambrosiani 75), Vicenza, 1984
G. Amelli, Un antichissimo codice biblico latino purpureo conservato nella chiesa di Sarezzano presso Tortona. Dissertazione critico-storica con note illustrative, Milan, 1872

projectDesc.p: This transcription was made by the Verbum Project as part of an online and printed edition of the Old Latin versions of John between 2003 and 2010.

editorialDecl.p: This transcription covers ff. 6r to 64v with omissions, based on Godu's edition. This manuscript covers John 1:8-4:29, 5:3-20, 5:29-7:45, 8:6-11:1, 11:12-34and was bound with VL 22A in antiquity.The transcription is based on Godu's edition, distinguishing between letters still visible and letters no longer visible. It includes foliation, lineation, all punctuation and abbreviations. We only provide reconstructed text for a line in which there is at least one letter extant, on the grounds that it is misleading to provide text for several or more lines which are completely missing. An exception is that we reconstruct partially extant words, and where a line begins and ends with such a word, text is supplied between them (cf. the line ae denuo in at 3.4, F 13v, Col. 2, l. 13).The initial transcription file was a plain text file for use with COLLATE. Further information about the procedures followed may be found in the electronic edition at
This second edition of the file was converted to XML by Catherine Smith using the project's Python conversion scripts 1.0.
The XML specifications used are version 1.5 of the IGNTP Guidelines available at

revisionDesc.change: Adjustment of transcription to version 1.5 of the XML by Hugh Houghton.
Reconversion of the original plain text file using Python scripts to version 1.4 of the XML by Catherine Smith and manual checking.
Hyphen entity replaced by lb attribute break="no" by Hugh Houghton
Changes made in order to display properly with VLI.xsl transformation by Hugh Houghton
Change to verse division of 5:18-19 by Hugh Houghton
Transcription converted to the TEI XML schema and corrected for parsing by Hugh Houghton

ab.note: Folios 5r and v are without legible text
Folios 6r column 2 and 6v column 1 are without legible text
Folios 6r column 2 and 6v column 1 are without legible text
Folios 7r and v are without legible text
Folio 8r column 2 is without legible text
Folios 10r column 2 and 10v column 1 are without legible text
There is no text on folios 20, 21, or 22.
Folios 25r and v are without legible text
On the basis of the other chapter numbers, we interpret this as Greek epsilon, although Godu reads a G
Ghiglione does not reconstruct 'illi' in this line
This chapter number is in Roman numerals
Godu's reconstruction of lines 3-6 is in smaller type. He comments (p. 78) 'J'ai suivi le texte du Veronensis qui permet de remplir au mieux les lignes'
There is no running title on this folio
Godu reads 'uoluntas', Ghiglione 'uolumtas'
There is no reading in other manuscripts which includes the letters visible here
Godu reads 'entem', Ghiglione 'tem'; both reconstructions differ from that offered here.
From here on the chapter numbers are in Roman numerals (and with the change from John 7:1 to John 7:2 follow a different system).
Folio 36v column 1 is without legible text

w.note: There is no running title on this folio

ab.note: Folios 39r column 2 and 39v column 1 are without legible text
Folios 40r and v are without legible text
Godu gives this chapter number as 'XG', but Ghiglione reads 'XVI'
Folios 44r column 2 and 44v column 1 are without legible text
Godu prints the word 'eius' in smaller font, noting that it is written small between the columns at the end of a full line.
Folio 45r column 2 is without legible text
Godu gives this chapter number as 'XGI', but Ghiglione reads 'XVII'
Folio 46r column 2 is without legible text
Godu restores this chapter number as 'XGII', but Ghiglione restores only the V in 'XVIII'
'die' is printed by Godu in smaller letters (compare John 8:44)

ab.w:
a
Godu reconstructs the last legible word as '[aet]a[tem], but it could equally be [ignor]a[mus]'.
iohannem·

ab.note: There is no reading in other manuscripts which includes the letters visible here
Folio 52r column 2 is without legible text
Folio 54r column 2 is without legible text
Folios 55r and v are without legible text
Folio 57r column 2 is without legible text
From Folio 58r to Folio 64v, the manuscript is mostly illegible: Godu's edition has been reproduced for these pages.
Folio 58r column 2 is without legible text
Folio 58v column 1 is without legible text
This 'aim' is possibly the end of 'ephraim' in John 11:54.
Folio 59v column 2 is without legible text
Folio 60r column 2 is without legible text
Folio 60v column 1 is without legible text
Folio 61r is without legible text
Folio 61v column 1 is without legible text
Folio 62r is without legible text
Folio 62v column 1 is without legible text
Folio 63r is without legible text
Folio 63v column 1 is without legible text
Folio 64r is without legible text
The running title is missing, although Godu records the decorative horizontal lines above and below.
Folio 64v column 1 is without legible text

This is a quire signature in the bottom margin. The current manuscript continues with a final quaternion from a different manuscript. This is treated as witness VL 22A, covering John 18:35-20:14 (with lacunae).
